---
title: "Black Highbush Blueberry (Vaccinium fuscatum)"
slug: "vaccinium-fuscatum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474732108/medium.jpgg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Black Highbush Blueberry"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Ericales"
tax_family: "Ericaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Ericales", "Ericaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474732108/medium.jpg" alt="Black Highbush Blueberry" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/willforestbeck">
willforestbeck</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264284215">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Ericales
-   **Family:** Ericaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=52739)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A deciduous shrub characterized by its slender branches, small urn-shaped white to pinkish flowers, and dark blue to black berries. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as swamps, bogs, and wet flatwoods, often in acidic soils. It blooms in early spring, and its fruits provide an important food source for birds and other wildlife.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/52739-Vaccinium-fuscatum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/1272)
